fect, we have
to keep in mind two factors of paramount importance in any
prognostication. The first is the dasa system of 120 years
which in itself is a system of directions. This is of great help in
fixing up the exact time for an event; and the nature of an
event can be read with the help of the dasa system and with
that of the directional aspects.
The second factor refers to the modification of events
brought forth by the transittingplanets. It has been our expe
rience that the influence of the transitting planets is capable
ofbeing read accurately only when we interpret the transits

Directions: Limitations 105
with reference to the radical Sun, Moon and Saturn, and also
with reference to the radical M.C. and ascendant. Even then,
as Mantreswara observed, Sun and Mars give results only
when they transit the first decanate of a sign; Venus and
Jupiter show their effects only when they are in the second
decanate; and Moon and Saturn have their influence as they
